journal_entry.description: Office relocation services provided to customer, payment due in 10 days
journal_entry.rationale: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services and earned revenue. Since payment is due in 10 days, this creates an Accounts Receivable (asset increase). The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one asset increase, and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) indicates one revenue increase, which correctly reflects this transaction. No tax information was provided in the lookup results, so no tax lines are added (the transaction may be between businesses where tax is handled separately, or the amount may already include tax). Total debits ($31,500) equal total credits ($31,500).

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided services to a customer, generating revenue. Since payment is due in 10 days (not received yet), the debit side would be Accounts Receivable (asset increase). The credit side is service revenue earned = revenue increase.
